source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Firewall Rule Changes

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That the Log Metric Filter and Alerts Exist for VPC Network Firewall Rule Changes
input_row.description: It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) Network Firewall rule changes.
input_row.rationale: Monitoring for Create or Update Firewall rule events gives insight to network access changes and may reduce the time it takes to detect suspicious activity. Impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. In the User-defined Metrics section, ensure at least one metric <Log_Metric_Name> is present with this filter text: resource.type="gce_firewall_rule" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.delete") Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 3. Go to Alerting by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerting. 4. Under the Policies section, ensure that at least one alert policy exists for the log metric above. Clicking on the policy should show that it is configured with a condition. For example, Violates when: Any logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name> stream is above a threshold of zero(0) for greater than zero(0) seconds means that the alert will trigger for any new owner change. Verify that the chosen alerting thresholds make sense for the user's organization. 5. Ensure that appropriate notification channels have been set up. From Google Cloud CLI Ensure that the prescribed log metric is present: 1. List the log metrics: gcloud logging metrics list --format json 2. Ensure that the output contains at least one metric with the filter set to: resource.type="gce_firewall_rule" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.delete") 3. Note the value of the property metricDescriptor.type for the identified metric, in the format logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>. Ensure that the prescribed alerting policy is present: 4. List the alerting policies: gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --format json 5. Ensure that the output contains an least one alert policy where: • conditions.conditionThreshold.filter is set to metric.type=\"logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>\" • AND enabled is set to true
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console Create the prescribed log metric: 1. Go to Logging/Logs-based Metrics by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics and click "CREATE METRIC". 2. Click the down arrow symbol on the Filter Bar at the rightmost corner and select Convert to Advanced Filter. 3. Clear any text and add: resource.type="gce_firewall_rule" AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.patch" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.insert" OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.delete")  4. Click Submit Filter. Display logs appear based on the filter text entered by the user. 5. In the Metric Editor menu on the right, fill out the name field. Set Units to 1 (default) and Type to Counter. This ensures that the log metric counts the number of log entries matching the advanced logs query. 6. Click Create Metric. Create the prescribed Alert Policy: 1. Identify the newly created metric under the section User-defined Metrics at https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/metrics. 2. Click the 3-dot icon in the rightmost column for the new metric and select Create alert from Metric. A new page displays. 3. Fill out the alert policy configuration and click Save. Choose the alerting threshold and configuration that makes sense for the user's organization. For example, a threshold of zero(0) for the most recent value ensures that a notification is triggered for every owner change in the project: Set `Aggregator` to `Count` Set `Configuration`: - Condition: above - Threshold: 0 - For: most recent value  4. Configure the desired notifications channels in the section Notifications. 5. Name the policy and click Save. From Google Cloud CLI Create the prescribed Log Metric • Use the command: gcloud logging metrics create Create the prescribed alert policy: • Use the command: gcloud alpha monitoring policies create References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/logs-based-metrics/ 2. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics/ 3. https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/alerts/ 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 5. https://cloud.google.com/vpc/docs/firewalls
input_row.impact: Enabling of logging may result in your project being charged for the additional logs usage. These charges could be significant depending on the size of the organization.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The control can be verified using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands, indicating it is Automated.
- The audit instructions provide specific gcloud CLI commands for listing log metrics and alerting policies.
- The required properties like metric filters and alert policies are standard GCP service properties for logging and monitoring.
- The description, remediation, and references confirm that the necessary checks can be accomplished via existing Google Cloud services and APIs without manual intervention.
- The fields provide a method to validate logging and monitoring configurations through programmatic queries.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_firewall_rule_change_alert_configuration

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **GCP API Calls/Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) Commands:**
   - Use `gcloud logging metrics list` to verify that a log metric exists with the filter set to:
     ```plaintext
     resource.type="gce_firewall_rule"
     AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.patch"
     OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.insert"
     OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.delete")
     ```
   - Use `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` to ensure that an alert policy exists with:
     - `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` matching `metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"`.
     - `enabled` property set to `true`.

2. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Enumerate firewall rule log metrics and corresponding alerting policies in the project.

3. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - **Pass:** At least one log-based metric matches the specified filter, and a corresponding alert policy is configured with the specified conditions.
   - **Fail:** No matching log metric or alert policy is found, indicating lack of monitoring for firewall rule changes.

4. **Expected Configuration Properties:**
   - A user-defined log metric for GCE firewall rule is present.
   - A monitoring policy alert condition linked to the log metric that triggers under specified thresholds is configured.

By verifying these specifications in an automated fashion, compliance with the control can be assured efficiently.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:**  
  The classification as **Automated** is correct. Both the existence of log-based metrics and alerting policies can be fully enumerated and validated using GCP APIs and Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) commands. No manual review of logs or console navigation is required.

- **GCP SDK/CLI Capabilities:**  
  - `gcloud logging metrics list` and `gcloud logging metrics describe <metric>` can enumerate and inspect log-based metrics.
  - `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` and `gcloud alpha monitoring policies describe <policy>` can enumerate and inspect alerting policies.
  - All required fields (filters, metric types, alert conditions) are accessible via these commands or corresponding REST APIs.

- **Other GCP Tools:**  
  - GCP does not have a direct equivalent to AWS Config or CloudFormation for this specific check, but the SDK/CLI/API approach is sufficient and standard.

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **8/10**

- **Strengths:**
  - The filter for the log metric is accurate and matches the CIS recommendation.
  - The use of `gcloud logging metrics list` is correct for enumerating metrics.
  - The use of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` is valid for alerting policies (though `alpha` may be replaced with `beta` or `GA` depending on current GCP CLI versions).
  - Pass/fail criteria are clear and technically sound.
  - The mapping between log metric and alert policy is well described.

- **Weaknesses:**
  - **gcloud Command Version:** `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` may be outdated; as of late 2023/2024, `gcloud monitoring policies list` is generally available and preferred over `alpha`.
  - **Metric Name Matching:** The check assumes the metric name is known or can be programmatically mapped from the filter. In practice, you may need to enumerate all user-defined metrics and match the filter string, as metric names are arbitrary.
  - **Alert Policy Condition:** The check for `conditions.conditionThreshold.filter` assumes a direct match to the metric, but the actual field may require parsing nested JSON/YAML.
  - **Program Name Convention:** The program name `gcp_logging_firewall_rule_change_alert_configuration` is descriptive, but could be more specific (see below).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Command Versioning:**  
  Use `gcloud monitoring policies list` (GA) instead of `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` unless a specific feature requires alpha.

- **Metric Filter Matching:**  
  Ensure the script/program checks all user-defined log metrics for the *exact* filter (not just by name), as users may use arbitrary metric names.

- **Alert Policy Mapping:**  
  The script should confirm that the alert policy’s condition is tied to the correct log metric by inspecting the `metric.type` or `filter` field in the alert policy’s condition.

- **Multi-Project/Org Scope:**  
  If auditing at scale, consider support for multiple projects or folders.

- **Program Name Suggestion:**  
  For clarity and consistency, consider:  
  `gcp_logging_vpc_firewall_rule_change_alert_configured`  
  or  
  `gcp_logging_vpc_firewall_rule_change_monitoring_enabled`

- **Missing Considerations:**  
  - **Alert Delivery:** The check does not confirm that the alert policy has a notification channel (e.g., email, Pub/Sub, SMS). For full compliance, ensure the alert is actionable.
  - **Policy State:** Confirm that the alert policy is enabled (`enabled: true`).
  - **Permissions:** The script/program should document required IAM permissions (e.g., `logging.metrics.list`, `monitoring.policies.list`).

- **Alternative API Methods:**  
  - Use the REST API endpoints for [Log Metrics](https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/v2/rest/v2/projects.metrics/list) and [Alert Policies](https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/api/ref_v3/rest/v3/projects.alertPolicies/list) for direct integration.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The assessment is technically sound and aligns with GCP best practices and CIS guidance.
- Minor improvements are recommended, but the fundamental approach is correct.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **MODIFY**

- **Keep the approach as Automated.**
- **Update the gcloud commands** to use GA versions where available.
- **Enhance the script/program** to:
  - Enumerate all log metrics and match the filter, not just by metric name.
  - Parse alert policy conditions to confirm linkage to the correct log metric.
  - Optionally, check for notification channels and enabled state.
- **Adopt a more specific program name** as suggested above.

---

#### **Summary Table**

| Section               | Assessment              |
|-----------------------|------------------------|
| Approach Validation   | AGREE                  |
| Technical Accuracy    | 8/10                   |
| Improvements          | See above              |
| Confidence            | HIGH                   |
| Final Recommendation  | MODIFY (see details)   |

---

**References:**
- [gcloud logging metrics list](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/logging/metrics/list)
- [gcloud monitoring policies list](https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/monitoring/policies/list)
- [CIS GCP Foundation Benchmark v4.0.0 - 2.7](https://workbench.cisecurity.org/benchmarks/10142)

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the existence and configuration of log-based metrics and alerting policies for VPC firewall rule changes can be fully validated using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). The required properties (metric filters, alerting policy conditions) are directly accessible via CLI/API without manual console navigation or subjective review. This control is a textbook example of an automated compliance check.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_gce_firewall_rule_change_alert_configuration

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Logging (Log-based Metrics), Monitoring (Alerting Policies)
- **API/CLI Method:**  
  - `gcloud logging metrics list`  
  - `gcloud logging metrics describe <metric_name>`  
  - `gcloud monitoring policies list` (or `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list` depending on SDK version)  
  - `gcloud monitoring policies describe <policy_id>`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - Log metric exists with filter:  
    ```
    resource.type="gce_firewall_rule"
    AND (protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.patch"
    OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.insert"
    OR protoPayload.methodName:"compute.firewalls.delete")
    ```
  - Alerting policy exists with a condition referencing the above log metric (`metric.type="logging.googleapis.com/user/<Log Metric Name>"`) and is enabled.
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - At least one log-based metric exists with the exact filter above.
  - At least one alerting policy is enabled and references the log-based metric.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No log-based metric with the required filter exists, OR
  - No enabled alerting policy references the required log-based metric.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements; both AI opinions supported automation. The final program name is made more descriptive for clarity and alignment with GCP resource naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
